skill.name: system-environment-setup
skill.description: Configure repeatable development and production-like environments with Docker Compose, environment files, dev containers, Makefile tasks, and Terraform examples.
skill.summary: Set up consistent project environments with Docker, environment variables, dev containers, and infrastructure templates.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly found many environment, shell, filesystem, network, and credential patterns, but the reviewed files are documentation templates. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found. Publish with warnings for placeholder secrets, destructive cleanup commands, and cloud infrastructure state guidance.

medium_findings.title: Placeholder Secrets and Credential Variables
medium_findings.description: The environment examples include database URLs, token secrets, SMTP credentials, Stripe keys, and AWS credential variable names. They are mostly placeholders and the guide says not to commit secrets, but users could copy weak defaults into real projects.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged values appear in a clearly labeled .env.example and are not sent anywhere. Risk remains because copied templates often become real configuration.

medium_findings.title: Shell Commands Can Change Local State
medium_findings.description: The Docker Compose and Makefile examples include service lifecycle commands, dependency installation, migration commands, and cleanup commands. These are legitimate for environment setup, but they can remove volumes or local build files if run without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are standard development tasks, not hidden execution logic. The destructive cleanup and volume removal examples create a real operational warning.

medium_findings.title: Cloud Infrastructure and State Handling Require Review
medium_findings.description: The Terraform example references AWS resources, remote state, and sensitive database variables. This is expected IaC content, but applying it can create cloud resources and Terraform state may contain sensitive values.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The IaC snippets are illustrative and incomplete, with no autonomous apply step. The risk is credible because Terraform state and cloud credentials are sensitive.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static prompt-injection alert maps to a PostgreSQL user configuration line. No text claims system authority, tells the evaluator to ignore instructions, or attempts to change the audit process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is ordinary Docker Compose configuration. I did not find prompt-injection language in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Network Findings Are Mostly Configuration References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs and addresses are localhost app configuration, SMTP host settings, a private CIDR block, and documentation links. No evidence found of data exfiltration or calls to an untrusted collection endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are visible setup examples and references. The SMTP example can enable outbound email in a real app, but the skill does not send mail.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Findings Are Relative Project References
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts are relative paths in dev container configuration and related-skill Markdown links. No runtime file read, write, or traversal primitive is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are static documentation examples. They do not process user input or access arbitrary filesystem locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to any selected algorithm or crypto implementation in the reviewed context. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar usage in operational code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose and Terraform variable declarations. The static match appears to be a heuristic false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment File and Secret Handling Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill teaches dotenv loading and required secret-like variables. This is normal environment setup guidance, but generated projects must avoid committing real values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is semantically present and intentional. The surrounding constraints reduce risk but do not remove the need for review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Cleanup Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The Makefile cleanup target removes build artifacts and dependencies. It is common in development workflows, but it can remove local files when copied into the wrong project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit and limited to named paths. It is still a destructive filesystem operation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Docker Volume Removal Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The Docker usage example includes removing containers and volumes. This can delete local database state if users run it against a project with valuable data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is a standard Docker Compose example. The risk depends on user context and warrants a marketplace warning.

dangerous_patterns.title: Terraform State and Sensitive Variable Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The Terraform examples reference remote state and sensitive database variables. State storage and variable handling should be reviewed before production use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is a legitimate Terraform setup. The security concern is real because state backends and sensitive variables often expose secrets if misconfigured.

content.value_statement: Inconsistent local setup slows onboarding and hides production differences. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft repeatable Docker, environment, dev container, Makefile, and Terraform setup plans.
actual_capabilities: Drafts Docker Compose layouts for web, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Nginx services.
Creates environment variable templates with gitignore guidance for local secret files.
Outlines Node dotenv loading and required variable validation patterns.
Defines VS Code dev container settings, extensions, ports, and post-create commands.
Provides Makefile task patterns for install, development, testing, Docker, migrations, and cleanup.
Sketches Terraform AWS resources, variables, and remote state structure.
limitations: Examples are generic and need project-specific review before production use.
Placeholder secrets and sample passwords must be replaced with secure secret management.
Terraform snippets are incomplete and omit full network, security group, and output definitions.
The skill does not verify installed tools, cloud accounts, or local machine state by itself.

use_cases.title: Bootstrap a New Web Project
use_cases.description: Create a repeatable local stack with application, database, cache, reverse proxy, and documented setup commands.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Onboarding
use_cases.description: Give new developers consistent dev container, environment variable, and Makefile workflows for faster setup.

use_cases.title: Plan Production-Like Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Draft Terraform and Docker patterns that mirror production dependencies before a full infrastructure review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Local Stack
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single application service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a local environment for my Node app with PostgreSQL and Redis. Include Docker Compose, required environment variables, and simple run commands.

prompt_templates.title: Add Team Onboarding Files
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for shared developer setup.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a team onboarding environment plan. Include .env.example guidance, .gitignore rules, a dev container, and a Makefile task list.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Existing Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user improving consistency and safety.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my existing environment setup. Identify missing services, unsafe defaults, secret handling gaps, and commands that need documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Design Production-Like Infrastructure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning across local and cloud environments.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft a production-like environment plan with Docker Compose for local development and Terraform structure for AWS. Flag security review items.

output_examples.input: Set up a local environment for a Node API with PostgreSQL and Redis.
output: A service plan for the app, database, cache, and reverse proxy.
An environment variable checklist with safe placeholder guidance.
A short command list for starting, stopping, and inspecting services.

output_examples.input: Prepare onboarding files for a new engineering team.
output: A dev container plan with editor extensions and forwarded ports.
A Makefile task outline for common development workflows.
A README checklist for installation, startup, testing, and troubleshooting.

output_examples.input: Plan AWS infrastructure for a production-like environment.
output: A Terraform structure covering provider setup, remote state, VPC, database, container cluster, and load balancer.
A list of security decisions that need human review before deployment.

best_practices: Replace every placeholder secret before running generated configuration in a real environment.
Review destructive commands and Docker volume behavior before sharing Makefile tasks with a team.
Run cloud infrastructure plans through security and cost review before applying Terraform.
anti_patterns: Copying sample passwords, token secrets, or cloud keys into production configuration.
Running cleanup or volume removal commands without checking the target project and data impact.
Treating the Terraform examples as complete production infrastructure without review.

faq.question: Can this skill create a complete production environment?
faq.answer: It can draft structure and examples, but production use needs security, reliability, and cost review.

faq.question: Does this skill store or transmit secrets?
faq.answer: No evidence found that the skill stores or transmits secrets. It does include secret placeholder examples.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes shell commands, environment variables, cleanup commands, and Terraform state examples that require careful use.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The generated report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Should generated .env files be committed?
faq.answer: No. Commit only safe examples and keep real local secret files out of version control.

faq.question: Are the Docker and Terraform snippets ready to run?
faq.answer: They are starting templates. Validate paths, versions, credentials, networking, and cloud settings before running them.
